• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Probability and random variables

Jorge Alonso

2026-05-21

1. Preparing, loading packages and data

# Loading packages
library(tidyverse)
# Cleaning memory
rm(list=ls(all=TRUE))
invisible(gc())
# Database link
processed_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jmalonso55/Applied-Statistics-for-a-Tropical-Arboretum/refs/heads/main/data/processed_data.csv"
# Loading data
data_trab <- read.csv(processed_url, sep = ",")

# Checking
summary(data_trab)
##    sec_cant             tombo         family             genus          
##  Length:4631        Min.   :   1   Length:4631        Length:4631       
##  Class :character   1st Qu.:2150   Class :character   Class :character  
##  Mode  :character   Median :4524   Mode  :character   Mode  :character  
##                     Mean   :4436                                        
##                     3rd Qu.:6644                                        
##                     Max.   :9180                                        
##    species              name            habito_pad            altura      
##  Length:4631        Length:4631        Length:4631        Min.   : 1.300  
##  Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   1st Qu.: 8.685  
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Median :13.060  
##                                                           Mean   :14.284  
##                                                           3rd Qu.:18.540  
##                                                           Max.   :44.350  
##       dap           densidade         n_caule         multicaule    
##  Min.   : 10.19   Min.   :0.1500   Min.   : 1.000   Min.   :0.0000  
##  1st Qu.: 23.30   1st Qu.:0.5200   1st Qu.: 1.000   1st Qu.:0.0000  
##  Median : 36.65   Median :0.5500   Median : 1.000   Median :0.0000  
##  Mean   : 44.43   Mean   :0.6026   Mean   : 1.466   Mean   :0.1486  
##  3rd Qu.: 60.16   3rd Qu.:0.7100   3rd Qu.: 1.000   3rd Qu.:0.0000  
##  Max.   :224.54   Max.   :1.2300   Max.   :42.000   Max.   :1.0000  
##  multicaule_l           rel_hd          area_basal      
##  Length:4631        Min.   :0.02199   Min.   :0.008155  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.:0.24818   1st Qu.:0.042639  
##  Mode  :character   Median :0.34983   Median :0.105511  
##                     Mean   :0.38235   Mean   :0.219827  
##                     3rd Qu.:0.48118   3rd Qu.:0.284253  
##                     Max.   :2.09473   Max.   :3.959837
# Filtering to remove NA from the family column
data_trab <- data_trab %>% 
  filter(!is.na(family))

2. Empirical probabilities

If an individual is randomly selected from the arboretum, what can we say about the probability of its family, stem status, or DBH class?

# Randomly sampling one individual
sample_n(data_trab, 1)
##   sec_cant tombo        family     genus species             name habito_pad
## 1      29B  5469 Anacardiaceae Mangifera  indica Mangifera indica       Tree
##   altura   dap densidade n_caule multicaule multicaule_l   rel_hd area_basal
## 1  17.45 62.71      0.55       1          0           No 0.278265  0.3088613
# If a plant is randomly selected in the arboretum, what is the probability that it is multi-stemmed?
total <- nrow(data_trab)
bif <- nrow(subset(data_trab, multicaule == 1))

p_evA <- bif/total
p_evA
## [1] 0.1484462
# Complementary event
p_evAc <- 1 - p_evA

p_evAc
## [1] 0.8515538
# Using dplyr
data_trab %>%
  summarise(p_multicaule = mean(multicaule == 1))
##   p_multicaule
## 1    0.1484462
# What is the probability that the individual is a palm?
p_evB <- data_trab %>%
  summarise(p_palmeira = mean(family == "Arecaceae")) %>% 
  pull()

p_evB
## [1] 0.3044936
# What is the probability of having DBH greater than or equal to 70 cm?
p_evC <- data_trab %>%
  summarise(p_dap70 = mean(dap >= 70)) %>% 
  pull()

p_evC
## [1] 0.168567
# Intersection event: What is the probability that the individual is a palm and has DBH greater than or equal to 70 cm?
p_B_inter_C <- data_trab %>%
  summarise(prob = mean(family == "Arecaceae" & dap >= 70)) %>% 
  pull()

p_B_inter_C
## [1] 0.04426559
# Union event: What is the probability that the individual is a palm or have a DBH greater than or equal to 70 cm?
(p_evB + p_evC) - p_B_inter_C
## [1] 0.428795
# Or:
p_B_union_C <- data_trab %>%
  summarise(prob = mean(family == "Arecaceae" | dap >= 70)) %>% 
  pull()

p_B_union_C
## [1] 0.428795
# Conditional probability: Among Fabaceae individuals, what is the probability of observing multi-stemmed individuals?
data_trab %>%
  filter(family == "Fabaceae") %>%
  summarise(p_bif_LEG = mean(multicaule == 1)) %>% 
  pull()
## [1] 0.1852825
# Conditional probability: Among multi-stemmed individuals, what is the probability of observing a plant from the Fabaceae family?
data_trab %>%
  filter(multicaule == 1) %>%
  summarise(p_bif_LEG = mean(family == "Fabaceae", na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  pull()
## [1] 0.2123494

3. Random variables

3.1. Binomial distribution

# Setting the number of trials (n) and the number of successes of interest (x)
n <- 20
x <- 3
# If 20 plants are randomly selected, what is the probability of observing exactly three multi-stemmed individuals?
dbinom(x, size = n, prob = p_evA)
## [1] 0.2427827
# What is the probability of observing three or fewer multi-stemmed plants in a sample of 20 plants?
pbinom(x, size = n, prob = p_evA, lower.tail = TRUE)
## [1] 0.6552638
# And what is the probability of observing more than three multi-stemmed plants in a sample of 20 plants?
pbinom(x, size = n, prob = p_evA, lower.tail = FALSE)
## [1] 0.3447362
# Plotting the probability distribution

# Defining the sample size and possible outcomes
n <- 20
x <- 0:n

# Calculating binomial probabilities
prob <- dbinom(x, size = n, prob = p_evA)

# Creating the data frame for plotting
dados_binom <- tibble(multicaule = x,  probabilidade = prob)

# Plotting the probability distribution
ggplot(dados_binom, aes(x = multicaule, y = probabilidade)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.3, fill = "lightblue") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, by = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Number of multi-stemmed individuals in a sample of 20", y = "Probability") +
  theme_classic()

# Estimating binomial parameters for multi-stemmed in a sample of 20 plants
binom_resumo <- tibble(n = n, p = p_evA, esperanca = n * p, variancia = n * p * (1 - p), desvio_padrao = sqrt(n * p * (1 - p)))

binom_resumo
## # A tibble: 1 × 5
##       n     p esperanca variancia desvio_padrao
##   <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>         <dbl>
## 1    20 0.148      2.97      2.53          1.59

3.2. Poisson distribution

Does the count of individuals with high DBH per planting bed behave as a Poisson variable?

# Calculating the 95th percentile for DBH
quantile(data_trab$dap, probs = c(0.95))
##    95% 
## 100.27
# Selecting the 50 beds with the highest number of plants
top_beds <- data_trab %>%
  count(sec_cant, name = "n_total", sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 50)

# Counting plants with DBH above the 95th percentile in each selected bed
bdap_beds <- data_trab %>%
  filter(sec_cant %in% top_beds$sec_cant) %>%
  group_by(sec_cant) %>%
  summarise(n_total = n(), n_dap = sum(dap > 100))

head(bdap_beds)
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   sec_cant n_total n_dap
##   <chr>      <int> <int>
## 1 10A           75     1
## 2 11H           52     5
## 3 13B           50     1
## 4 14A          105     5
## 5 16G           32     8
## 6 17B           33     2
# Checking mean, variance, and dispersion
bdap_beds %>%
  summarise(mean_n_dap = mean(n_dap), var_n_dap = var(n_dap), dispersion_ratio = var_n_dap / mean_n_dap)
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   mean_n_dap var_n_dap dispersion_ratio
##        <dbl>     <dbl>            <dbl>
## 1       2.14      4.82             2.25

The Poisson distribution can be used as a theoretical reference, but the data show overdispersion. Therefore, the Poisson distribution does not perfectly describe this pattern.

# Theoretical Poisson distribution

# Estimating the Poisson parameter
lambda <- mean(bdap_beds$n_dap)

# Generating theoretical Poisson probabilities
poisson_theoretical <- tibble(n_dap = 0:max(bdap_beds$n_dap), probability = dpois(n_dap, lambda = lambda))

poisson_theoretical
## # A tibble: 9 × 2
##   n_dap probability
##   <int>       <dbl>
## 1     0     0.118  
## 2     1     0.252  
## 3     2     0.269  
## 4     3     0.192  
## 5     4     0.103  
## 6     5     0.0440 
## 7     6     0.0157 
## 8     7     0.00480
## 9     8     0.00128
# Plotting the theoretical Poisson distribution
ggplot(poisson_theoretical, aes(x = n_dap, y = probability)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.4, fill = "lightblue") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:max(poisson_theoretical$n_dap)) +
  labs(x = "Number of plants with DBH > 100 cm per bed", y = "Probability") +
  theme_classic()

3.3. Normal distribution

# Filtering for Euphorbiaceae
euphorbiaceae <- data_trab %>% 
  filter(family == "Euphorbiaceae")
# QQ-plot for height values
qqnorm(euphorbiaceae$altura)
qqline(euphorbiaceae$altura, col = "red")

# Normality test
shapiro.test(euphorbiaceae$altura)
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  euphorbiaceae$altura
## W = 0.99025, p-value = 0.633
# Estimating parameters
mean_altura <- mean(euphorbiaceae$altura)
sd_altura <- sd(euphorbiaceae$altura)

mean_altura
## [1] 15.81222
sd_altura
## [1] 5.665515
# Calculating the empirical probability of height > 20 m among Euphorbiaceae
euphorbiaceae %>% 
  summarise(n_total = n(), n_altura_20 = sum(altura > 20, na.rm = TRUE), prob_empirica = mean(altura > 20, na.rm = TRUE))
##   n_total n_altura_20 prob_empirica
## 1     108          23      0.212963
# Calculating the theoretical probability of Euphorbiaceae height > 20 m assuming normality
pnorm(q = 20, mean = mean_altura, sd = sd_altura, lower.tail = FALSE)
## [1] 0.2299019
# Calculating the 2.5% and 97.5% quantiles
q_lower <- qnorm(0.025, mean = mean_altura, sd = sd_altura)
q_upper <- qnorm(0.975, mean = mean_altura, sd = sd_altura)

q_lower
## [1] 4.708017
q_upper
## [1] 26.91643
# Creating values for the normal curve
normal_curve <- tibble(altura = seq(from = mean_altura - 4 * sd_altura, to = mean_altura + 4 * sd_altura, length.out = 1000), density = dnorm(altura, mean = mean_altura, sd = sd_altura)) %>%
  mutate(tail = case_when(altura <= q_lower ~ "Lower 2.5%", altura >= q_upper ~ "Upper 2.5%", TRUE ~ "Central 95%"))

# Plotting
ggplot(normal_curve, aes(x = altura, y = density)) +
  geom_area(data = filter(normal_curve, tail == "Lower 2.5%"), fill = "red", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_area(data = filter(normal_curve, tail == "Upper 2.5%"), fill = "red", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = q_lower, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = q_upper, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(round(q_lower, 2), round(q_upper, 2))) +
  labs(x = "Height (m)", y = "Density") +
  theme_classic()
